text.title: YouTube Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every YouTube Strategy application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Channel MUST commit to a niche (audience + topic + angle) in one sentence. Generic channels are rejected.
rule.rationale: Algorithm and audience both reward niche specificity; generic channels never break threshold.

rule.statement: First 20 videos MUST target search-intent keywords (tutorials, how-to). Pure discovery content is rejected before subscriber base.
rule.rationale: Search videos compound for years; discovery videos depend on hot algorithm slots that small channels rarely earn.

rule.statement: Every video MUST land its hook in the first 15 seconds. Logo intros and 'before we start' preambles are rejected.
rule.rationale: Retention curve drops sharply past 15 seconds; hook position determines reach.

rule.statement: Cadence MUST be sustainable for ≥6 months without burnout (≤1 video/week recommended for solos). Bi-weekly bursts are rejected.
rule.rationale: YouTube rewards consistency; burnout drops cause the algorithm to deprioritise the channel.

rule.statement: Each video MUST ship with thumbnail + title pair tested as a unit. Mismatched thumb/title pairs are rejected.
rule.rationale: CTR depends on thumb + title together; testing them independently misses the interaction effect.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
